description: AsyncCircuitBreaker is built but never wired into AsyncChannelPool or router; call() method at line 45 is never invoked; _record_success/_record_failure are private preventing external integration
proposed_fix: Add public async record_success() and record_failure() methods; wire CircuitBreaker around AsyncChannelPool.acquire(); invoke .call() as wrapper in middleware layer; update build_channel_pool to accept optional circuit_breaker

description: StandardDnsResolver is built via factory but never injected into SessionRouterAdapter or CommunicationFabricAssembler; endpoints remain hardcoded in config and DNS-based discovery has no effect
proposed_fix: Inject dns_resolver into SessionRouterAdapter as a fallback source; when resolve() gets no session match
column10: attempt DNS resolution before returning None

description: ConfigServiceResolver is built but never injected into the assembly; YAML-defined services have no impact on routing — documentation and runtime behavior diverge
proposed_fix: Inject config_resolver into SessionRouterAdapter as a fallback endpoint source; merge YAML-defined endpoints with runtime session map

description: AsyncChannelPool.acquire() calls TransportFactory.create_channel() at line 49 without asyncio.wait_for(); transient failures or connection hangs block acquire() forever; no connect retry mechanism exists
proposed_fix: Add connect_timeout via asyncio.wait_for() and connect_retry_count to AsyncChannelPool.__init__() and acquire(); classify transient failures and retry before raising

description: asyncio.Lock() created at line 16 but never acquired in select(); self._index += 1 at line 28 is not protected by the lock making it unsafe under concurrency
proposed_fix: Remove the unused self._lock entirely (simplest fix) or acquire it in select() to protect the index increment

description: resolve() at line 38 compares channel.state.value against string literals "active"/"connecting" instead of using ChannelState enum — silent breakage if enum values change
proposed_fix: Replace hardcoded strings with channel.state in (ChannelState.ACTIVE

description: Lines 15-17 contain duplicate imports (from typing import List

description: resolve() at line 52 uses list comprehension [e if e.port != port else ...] that returns ALL endpoints regardless of port — port filter has no effect and callers get unexpected results
proposed_fix: Either rewrite with a proper filter [e for e in eps if e.port == port] or remove the port parameter if filtering is not intended

description: _record_success() (line 68) and _record_failure() (line 79) are private methods; external code such as middleware or higher-layer interceptors cannot call them
column10: Add public async record_success() and async record_failure() methods that delegate to the private implementations; update __all__ if needed
